etymology.description: semantic 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic 丩(丩) -> shout
etymology.interpretation: The mouth 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") indicates an action related to speaking or making noise, while the phonetic 丩(丩) suggests the sound of shouting or calling. Together, they convey the concept of calling out or shouting.

examples.chinese: 他叫了一声(他叫了一聲)，但没有人回答(但沒有人回答)。
examples.pinyin: Tā jiào le yī shēng, dàn méiyǒu rén huídá.
examples.english: He shouted, but no one answered.

examples.chinese: 我叫他来帮忙(我叫他來幫忙)，他马上就答应了(他馬上就答應了)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ jiào tā lái bāngmáng, tā mǎshàng jiù dāyìng le.
examples.english: I called him to help, and he agreed immediately.

examples.chinese: 医生叫我每天多喝水(醫生叫我每天多喝水)。
examples.pinyin: Yīshēng jiào wǒ měitiān duō hē shuǐ.
examples.english: The doctor told me to drink more water every day.
